Implementation Actions: • Train admissions staff on unconscious bias and inclusive practices • Ensure application forms do not request unnecessary information that could bias decisions (e.g., parent occupation, family income) • Monitor admissions data for patterns of exclusion (e.g., disproportionately low acceptance rates for certain groups) Grievance Mechanism: • Provide clear process for families to raise concerns about discriminatory treatment • Investigate complaints promptly and impartially • Report findings to Yayasan Board and accreditation bodies as required
content.title: Non-Discrimination Policies

content.text: CIS/WASC Standard: Community Engagement Schools should actively engage with prospective families and local community: Outreach Strategies: • Host open houses and campus tours (quarterly) • Participate in education fairs and community events • Offer presentations at feeder schools (primary schools for secondary admissions) • Engage alumni as ambassadors (share their success stories) Accessibility Considerations: • Ensure school facilities are accessible for families with disabilities (ramps, elevators, accessible restrooms) • Provide bilingual admissions materials (English and Bahasa Indonesia) • Offer flexible meeting times (evenings, weekends) for working parents • Consider financial aid or sliding scale tuition for families who cannot afford full fees Community Partnerships: • Collaborate with local businesses for scholarship sponsorship • Partner with community organizations to identify underserved students who would benefit from Cambridge education • Offer scholarships for high-achieving students from low-income backgrounds Evidence for Accreditation: • Records of open houses and campus tours (attendance, feedback) • Financial aid recipients (demonstrate socioeconomic diversity) • Community partnership agreements (MOUs with sponsoring organizations)
content.title: Community Engagement and Accessibility

content.text: CIS/WASC Standard: Integrity in Communications Schools must ensure all marketing and communications are: 1. Accurate: \- Claims about facilities, programs, results are verifiable \- Do not exaggerate or embellish 2. Transparent: \- Clearly state what is included in tuition and what costs extra \- Acknowledge limitations (e.g., "We are a growing school; A Level program launches in 2027") 3. Ethical: \- Do not denigrate competing schools \- Respect privacy of students and families (obtain consent for photos/videos) \- Avoid manipulative or high-pressure sales tactics Evidence for Accreditation: • Marketing materials approval log (who approves content before publication) • Website audit (verify accuracy of information) • Parent consent forms for use of student images • Complaints log (track and resolve any grievances about misleading information) For more details on marketing ethics, see Section 11.6.
content.title: Marketing Ethics and Truth in Advertising

content.text: Content: • Mission and values alignment • Non-discrimination statement • Admissions criteria by year level • Placement assessment procedures • EAL and SEN support policies • Tuition and fee structure • Enrollment process and timelines • Refund and withdrawal policies • Appeals process Format: • Written in clear, accessible language (EAL-friendly) • Available in English and Bahasa Indonesia • Published on the school website and provided in the admissions packet Review Cycle: • Annual review (before admissions season) • Approved by Principal, General Manager, and Yayasan Board • Version controlled (track changes and updates) Storage Location: • EduNav → School Documents → Admissions folder • Hard copy in Principal's office and Admissions Coordinator's office
content.title: Admissions Policy Document

content.text: Forms to Maintain: 1. Application Form: \- Student information, family background, educational history \- Bilingual (English and Bahasa Indonesia) \- Available in hard copy and digital (fillable PDF) 2. Document Checklist: \- Lists all required documents (birth certificate, transcripts, photos, etc.) \- Parents check off as they submit each item 3. Medical Information Form: \- Health history, allergies, medications, emergency contacts 4. Photo and Video Consent Form: \- Separate consent for internal vs. external use 5. Data Privacy Consent Form: \- General data processing, third-party sharing, retention Storage: • Templates stored in shared drive (accessible to admissions staff) • Completed forms filed in individual student admissions folders • Digital copies uploaded to student profile in EduNav
content.title: Application Forms and Checklists

content.text: Assessment Tools: 1. English Language Assessment: \- Reading comprehension passages (3 levels: primary, lower secondary, upper secondary) \- Writing prompts with scoring rubric (grammar, vocabulary, organization) \- Speaking interview guide (questions and CEFR rating scale) \- Listening comprehension audio files and questions 2. Mathematics Assessment: \- Age-appropriate problem sets (Years 1-12) \- Answer keys and scoring guides \- Diagnostic analysis template (identify specific skill gaps) 3. Subject Screening (for IGCSE/A Level entrants): \- Science: Biology, Chemistry, Physics topic quizzes \- Business: Case study analysis \- Geography, History: Short-answer questions Rubrics: • CEFR placement rubric (A1 to C1 descriptors) • Mathematics skills checklist (number sense, algebra, geometry, etc.) • Subject readiness indicators (prerequisites for IGCSE/A Level courses) Review and Updates: • Subject Leaders review assessment tools annually • Ensure alignment with current Cambridge curriculum • Adjust difficulty level based on student performance data Storage: • Secure digital folder (password-protected) • Hard copies in locked cabinet (prevent cheating if students share answers) CEFR Language Assessment Rubric Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) Levels: Assessment Criteria: Scoring: • Each skill assessed on 1-6 scale (aligned with CEFR levels) • Overall CEFR level determined by average across four skills Use: • Place EAL students in appropriate support tier (see Section 8.4) • Track language development over time (reassess every 6-12 months) Enrollment Contract Template Key Sections (see Section 11.4 for full details): 1. Parties (School and Parent/Guardian) 2. Student Information (name, year level, start date) 3. Tuition and Fees (amount, payment schedule) 4. School Responsibilities (quality education, safeguarding, communication) 5. Parent Responsibilities (attendance, homework support, payment, policy adherence) 6. Refund and Withdrawal Policy 7. Dispute Resolution (mediation, arbitration, applicable law) 8. Signatures and Date Legal Review: • Contract template reviewed by Yayasan legal counsel annually • Compliant with Indonesian contract law • Enforceable in case of disputes Storage: • Signed original in student admissions file (hard copy) • Scanned copy in EduNav (digital) • Master template in shared drive (updated annually) Parent Orientation Presentation Content (Bilingual Slides): 1. Welcome and Introductions 2. School Mission, Vision, and Values 3. Cambridge Curriculum Overview 4. Academic Expectations and Homework Policy 5. Student Support Services (EAL, SEN, counseling) 6. Communication Channels (EduNav, email, parent-teacher conferences) 7. Safeguarding and Wellbeing 8. Co-Curricular and Extracurricular Programs 9. Uniforms, Logistics, and Daily Routines 10. Questions and Answers Delivery: • In-person session (1-2 hours) before term starts • Record session for families who cannot attend • Provide printed handout or digital PDF of key points Updates: • Refresh annually with current information (calendar, staff, programs) • Incorporate feedback from previous orientation evaluations Storage: • Presentation file in shared drive → Admissions → Orientation folder • Attendance records (for compliance tracking)
content.title: Placement Test Instruments and Rubrics

content.text: Types of Consent (see Section 11.9 for details): 1. General Data Processing Consent 2. Photo and Video Consent (internal vs. external use) 3. Medical Treatment Authorization 4. Third-Party Data Sharing (Cambridge, Dapodik, insurance) Form Elements: • Clear description of data use • Opt-in checkboxes (parents actively consent) • Signature and date • Option to withdraw consent at any time Storage: • Signed consent forms in student admissions file (hard copy) • Digital copy in EduNav linked to student profile • Master templates in shared drive Audit: • Annual audit of consent forms to ensure all students have current, valid consents • Update consents if policies change
content.title: Data Privacy Consent Forms

content.text: • Track all marketing materials before publication • Ensure accuracy, ethical messaging, and compliance with Cambridge branding guidelines Log Entries Include: Approval Process: 1. Creator drafts material 2. Submit to Admissions Coordinator for initial review (fact-check) 3. Submit to Principal for final approval 4. For major campaigns: GM or Yayasan Board approval required 5. Once approved, material published and logged Storage: • Approval log maintained in shared spreadsheet (Google Sheets or Excel) • Copies of all approved materials archived in shared drive → Marketing folder
content.title: Marketing Materials Approval Log

content.text: Information Tracked: Process: • Update waitlist monthly (as spaces open or applicants withdraw) • Contact waitlisted families with status updates (every 2-3 months) • Prioritize based on established criteria (sibling, date of application, specific needs) Storage: • Waitlist spreadsheet in shared drive → Admissions folder • Access restricted to Admissions Coordinator and Principal Exit Interview Summary Reports Reporting: • Quarterly summary of exit interview themes shared with Principal and SLT • Use insights for continuous improvement (e.g., if multiple families cite "communication issues," address in professional development) Storage: • Individual exit interview notes in confidential folder (access restricted to Principal and Admissions Coordinator) • Aggregated summary reports in shared drive
content.title: Waitlist Management Records

content.text: Confidentiality:

items: Appeals records are highly sensitive
Stored in secure, access-restricted folder
Only Principal, GM, and involved staff have access
content.title: Appeals Decision Records

items: Track NISN for all enrolled students
Ensure 100% NISN registration for Indonesian citizens
Identify and resolve any NISN issues (duplicates, errors)

content.text: Updates:

items: Dapodik operator updates sheet monthly
Cross-check with Dapodik system quarterly

content.text: All admissions evidence and artifacts stored in: Digital: • EduNav → School Documents → Admissions folder (primary location) • Shared drive (Google Drive or OneDrive) → Admissions folder (backup and collaborative access) Physical: • Admissions office → Locked filing cabinets (organized by academic year and student name) • Principal's office → Archived records (past years, long-term retention) Responsible Party: Primary: Admissions Coordinator Secondary: Principal Support: Administrative staff (data entry, filing) Review Cycle: • Annually (Before Admissions Season): \- Review and update Admissions Policy \- Refresh application forms and assessment tools \- Update marketing materials \- Conduct audit of previous year's admissions data • Quarterly: \- Review waitlist status \- Analyze exit interview trends \- Verify NISN and Dapodik accuracy • As Needed: \- Update consent forms when policies change \- Revise enrollment contracts when tuition changes
content.title: Storage Location:

content.text: Overall Policy Oversight: • Develop and maintain Admissions Policy in collaboration with General Manager and Yayasan • Ensure policy aligns with school mission, Indonesian regulations, Cambridge standards, and CIS/WASC criteria • Approve all major admissions decisions, including rejections and appeals Final Decisions on Appeals: • Review appeal submissions and supporting documentation • Conduct appeal meetings with families • Make final decision on admission, waitlist, or rejection (in consultation with GM) Admissions Standards: • Set criteria for academic readiness, EAL support, and SEN accommodations • Monitor admissions data (acceptance rates, demographic diversity, student outcomes) • Ensure non-discrimination and equity in admissions process Marketing Approval: • Review and approve all marketing materials (brochures, website content, social media posts) • Ensure truth in advertising and ethical communications Stakeholder Communication: • Communicate admissions outcomes to prospective families (acceptance, rejection, waitlist) • Represent school at admissions events (open houses, education fairs) • Build relationships with feeder schools and community partners
content.title: Principal

content.text: Day-to-Day Process Management: 1. Inquiry Management:

items: Respond to initial inquiries (phone, email, walk-ins)
Provide information about school, programs, tuition, admissions process
Schedule campus tours and interviews
content.title: Admissions Coordinator

content.text: 2. Application Processing: \- Receive and review application forms \- Verify completeness of required documents \- Coordinate placement assessments (schedule tests, administer assessments) \- Compile application files for review by Principal and Subject Leaders 3. Enrollment Coordination: \- Prepare acceptance letters and enrollment contracts \- Collect tuition deposits and signed contracts \- Coordinate new family orientation (schedule, logistics, materials) \- Enter student data into EduNav and Dapodik (or coordinate with IT staff) 4. Waitlist Management: \- Maintain waitlist records \- Communicate with waitlisted families (updates, status changes) \- Notify families when spaces become available 5. Exit Procedures: \- Conduct exit interviews with withdrawing families \- Prepare student records for transfer \- Process refunds (coordinate with Finance Office) 6. Record-Keeping: \- Maintain organized admissions files (digital and physical) \- Track admissions metrics (applications, acceptances, enrollments, withdrawals) \- Prepare admissions reports for Principal and accreditation purposes 7. Marketing Support:

items: Draft marketing materials (with Principal approval)
Update school website and social media with admissions information
Coordinate admissions events (open houses, tours)

content.text: Placement Testing and Assessment: 1. Subject Leaders: \- Develop and review placement test instruments (English, Mathematics, Science) \- Score assessments and interpret results \- Provide recommendations for year level placement and support needs 2. EAL Coordinator: \- Conduct CEFR language assessments \- Determine EAL support tier (1, 2, or 3) \- Advise on language development timeline and goals 3. Learning Support Coordinator: \- Review SEN documentation (IEPs, psychological assessments) \- Assess school's capacity to support student's needs \- Recommend accommodations and interventions 4. Homeroom Teachers (for student interviews): \- Conduct informal interviews with prospective students (Years 4+) \- Assess social-emotional readiness and fit \- Provide input on student's strengths and potential challenges Curriculum and Instruction: 5. Bridging and Catch-Up Support: \- Develop catch-up plans for transfer students with curriculum gaps \- Provide additional support during transition period (first term) 6. New Student Orientation: \- Introduce new students to classmates and routines \- Facilitate social integration (buddy system, clubs)
content.title: Academic Staff

content.text: Fee Structure Approval: • Review and approve annual tuition and fee schedule (in consultation with Principal and Yayasan) • Ensure financial sustainability while maintaining accessibility Contract Terms: • Review enrollment contract for legal compliance • Approve refund policies and payment terms Policy Alignment: • Ensure admissions practices align with Eduversal standards across all partner schools • Facilitate sharing of best practices among schools Appeals Review: • Consult on complex or contentious admissions cases • Provide final approval for appeals decisions (alongside Principal) Strategic Oversight: • Monitor enrollment trends and capacity planning • Support marketing and outreach initiatives (budget approval, strategic direction)
content.title: General Manager

content.text: Data Entry and Portal Setup: 1. Student Profile Creation: \- Enter new student data into EduNav (demographics, contact information, enrollment details) \- Assign student ID number and homeroom \- Generate parent portal login credentials 2. Dapodik Entry: \- Input student data into Dapodik system (Indonesian government database) \- Request and verify NISN (national student ID number) \- Update Dapodik each semester with enrollment status 3. Data Security: \- Ensure student data is stored securely with appropriate access controls \- Regularly backup data \- Monitor for data breaches or unauthorized access 4. Parent Portal Training: \- Assist parents with logging into EduNav during orientation \- Troubleshoot technical issues (forgotten passwords, access problems) \- Provide user guides in English and Bahasa Indonesia 5. Reporting and Analytics: \- Generate admissions reports (applications, enrollments, demographics) \- Track key metrics (acceptance rate, yield rate, retention rate) \- Support data-driven decision-making for admissions strategy ## CROSS-REFERENCES This section connects with:

items: Section 2 (Indonesian Compliance): PPDB requirements, NISN registration, Dapodik reporting
Section 3 (Cambridge Centre Compliance): Candidate registration, exam entry deadlines
Section 5 (Curriculum and Academic Program): Curriculum alignment for transfer students, subject prerequisites
Section 8 (Inclusion, EAL, and Learning Support): EAL placement, CEFR levels, MTSS framework, SEN accommodations, IEP development
Section 9 (Safeguarding): Safer recruitment checks, safeguarding briefing for parents, risk assessment for boarding admissions
Section 10 (Cambridge Examinations Management): Exam entry deadlines, candidate information accuracy
Section 14 (Data Protection): Privacy policies, consent forms, data retention schedules, incident response
content.title: IT/EduNav Administrator

content.text: ## COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST Indonesian Compliance: ☑ Admissions policy published in Bahasa Indonesia ☑ PPDB committee formed with clear roles ☑ Non-discrimination policy in place ☑ NISN registration within 30 days of enrollment ☑ Dapodik updated three times per year ☑ Age requirements align with Permendikbudristek regulations ☑ BAN-S/M admissions standards met (documentation, equity, capacity) Cambridge Compliance: ☑ Candidate information accurate (legal name, date of birth) ☑ Exam entries submitted by deadlines (February for June, August for November) ☑ Mid-year enrollment families informed of exam timeline constraints ☑ Cambridge logo and branding used correctly in marketing CIS/WASC Compliance: ☑ Written admissions policy (fair, transparent, consistently applied) ☑ Non-discrimination policy explicit and implemented ☑ Marketing materials accurate and approved ☑ Parent consent obtained for photo/video use ☑ Admissions decisions documented with rationale ☑ Appeals process clear and accessible ☑ Community engagement and outreach activities ☑ Financial aid or scholarship opportunities (if feasible) Data Protection: ☑ GDPR-aligned data practices (lawfulness, transparency, security) ☑ Parent consent for data processing, sharing, and use of images ☑ Data retention schedule implemented ☑ Secure storage and access controls for student records ☑ Incident response plan for data breaches ## CONCLUSION Section 11 provides comprehensive guidance for Eduversal Partner School Principals and admissions teams to:

items: Conduct fair, transparent, and compliant admissions processes
Support diverse learners (EAL, SEN, various academic backgrounds)
Communicate ethically and accurately with prospective families
Protect student data and privacy
Align with Indonesian regulations, Cambridge standards, and CIS/WASC criteria
